# Docs As Code

## Summary

When documentation is part of the product contract or operating model, code and docs should move together.

## Add Or Update Docs In The Same Change When

- a product claim changes
- a workflow changes
- a runbook step changes
- a security or trust boundary changes
- a public-facing behavior changes
- a verification contract changes

## Structure Expectations

- prefer smaller linked documents over one long undifferentiated page when the material covers multiple tasks or audiences
- use task-based headings where possible
- put short orienting text under headings so the reader knows why the section exists
- keep documentation scannable with clear summaries, headings, and navigation support

## Single Source And Status

- prefer single-source documentation over duplicated near-copies when the same truth must serve multiple versions or audiences
- mark which docs are canonical, reference-only, historical, or superseded
- avoid creating parallel docs that drift unless the product or version genuinely requires separate content

## Stale Documentation Cleanup

Use [[Cleanup And Stale Artifact Discipline]] when docs contradict current repo truth or when older docs remain after a workflow, product behavior, or architecture changed.

- identify canonical truth before editing or deleting docs
- update or delete docs in the same change when code or behavior changes
- mark historical docs as historical, superseded, or archive-only
- do not let stale docs remain partially authoritative
- delete or merge duplicate docs when one stronger canonical source exists

## Canonical Vs Reference Docs

Mark which docs are:

- canonical execution docs
- reference-only context
- historical or superseded material

Agents should not treat every markdown file in a repo as equally current.

## Lint And Review

- documentation quality should be checked with linting or review discipline, not left to chance
- if the repo has a docs lint step, run it
- if docs are versioned or conditional, keep the versioning rules in the source rather than duplicating whole files
- treat broken structure, stale versioning, and obvious scannability regressions as real defects

## Shared Wiki Rule

Promote only reusable guidance into this vault.

Keep product truth in the project repo.
